method_steps: PREP (day prior): Pat shoulders completely dry. Season aggressively on all surfaces with salt flakes and cracked pepper. Allow to sit uncovered in coldroom overnight on a rack to dry the surface — non-negotiable for a proper sear.

SEAR: Bring shoulder to room temperature 30–45 min before cooking. Heat a large rondeau or heavy-base roasting pan over high heat until smoking. Add olive oil. Sear shoulders fat-side down first for 4–5 min until deep mahogany — do not lift early. Turn and sear all faces. Total sear 12–15 min. Remove to tray.

BRAISE BASE: In the same pan, reduce heat to medium. Sweat onion, celery, carrot and halved garlic head 8–10 min until soft and beginning to colour. Add tomato paste, stir and cook 2–3 min until it darkens and sticks slightly — this is the umami base. Deglaze with red wine, scraping all fond from the pan. Reduce wine by half (approx 10–12 min at a steady simmer).

BRAISE: Return shoulders to pan fat-side up. Pour hot stock around — liquid should reach halfway up the meat, not cover it. Add rosemary, thyme, bay leaves. Bring to a simmer on stove. Cover tightly with lid or two layers of foil crimped at edges. Place in oven at 155°C (fan-forced) or 165°C (conventional). Cook 4–4.5 hours. Check at 3.5 hours — meat should be pulling away at bone and yielding completely to a probe.

PROVENÇALE VEG: While lamb braises, heat olive oil in a wide sauté pan over high heat. Sear capsicum and fennel in a single layer until charred edges develop, 4–5 min. Add zucchini, cook a further 2 min. Season with salt, herbes de Provence. Remove from heat — veg should have colour and texture, not be stewed soft. Hold separately.

RESTING & PULLING: Remove shoulders from oven. Lift carefully onto a tray lined with a rack. Rest uncovered 20 min minimum. Pull meat into large irregular chunks 60–80g using two forks — keep pieces generous, not shredded. Reserve any escaped juices.

JUS: Strain braising liquid through a fine chinois into a wide saucepan, pressing aromatics firmly. Skim fat from surface. Reduce over medium-high heat to a loose coating consistency (approx 30–40% reduction). Add reserved pulling juices. Taste and correct salt. Add olives in the final 5 min of reduction only — do not boil olives long or they turn bitter. Adjust with a small squeeze of lemon if jus reads flat.

GREMOLATA: Combine fine-chopped parsley, lemon zest and microplaned raw garlic. Bind with a thread of extra virgin olive oil. Season lightly. Make no more than 2 hours before service — garlic turns acrid if left too long.
HOLD: Return pulled lamb chunks to jus in a deep GN tray. Provençale veg held separately. Hold lamb in jus in bain marie at 78–80°C.
service: GN EVENT: Spoon lamb chunks into warm bowls or onto plates. Add a spoonful of provençale veg alongside. Ladle jus generously over — lamb must not look dry. Finish with a teaspoon of gremolata placed on top, not stirred in.
PLATE SERVICE: 160–180g pulled lamb per portion. Pool jus in base of bowl first, place lamb, veg to one side, gremolata on top. Do not flatten — rustic height reads better.
Hold temp: bain marie at 78–80°C. Do not hold above 85°C or the pulled meat will continue to cook and dry. Check jus level every 30 min — add a splash of warm stock if reducing.
note: Bone-in shoulder is non-negotiable — collagen and marrow from the blade enrich the jus considerably. Tomato paste in the braise base is the structural umami anchor. Olives added only at jus finish: extended heat makes them bitter. Gremolata raw garlic is powerful — do not prepare more than 2 hours ahead. Celery added to the mirepoix — the original lacked the third aromatic leg. 4.5 kg raw bone-in yields approximately 2.2–2.5 kg pulled meat at 110–125g cooked per portion.

name: Beef Bourguignon
subtitle: Braised Beef Chuck · Smoked Lardons · Pearl Onions · Button Mushrooms · Burgundy Sauce
label: Beef Bourguignon

ingredients.prep: cut into 60–70g cubes (approx 5–6cm) — trimmed of sinew but intramuscular fat left intact
quartered — do not wash, wipe with damp cloth
peeled — blanch 1 min to loosen skins

ingredients.item: Beef Stock or Brown Veal Stock

ingredients.prep: rough 3cm chop — braise base only, strained out
rough 3cm chop — braise base only, strained out
bay leaf, thyme sprigs, parsley stalks tied in leek or muslin
unsalted, divided — 20g for pearl onions, 20g for mushrooms

method_steps: MARINADE (optional, recommended): Combine beef cubes, wine, rough onion, carrot, garlic and bouquet garni. Refrigerate 12–24 hours. If marinating, drain beef thoroughly and pat dry before searing — wet beef will steam, not sear.

LARDONS: Blanch lardons in cold water, bring to boil, drain — removes excess salt. Render in a dry heavy pot over medium heat until fat releases and edges crisp, 6–8 min. Remove with slotted spoon; reserve fat in pot.

SEAR BEEF: Working in small batches of 400–500g maximum — never crowd the pan. Pat beef completely dry. Sear in lardon fat plus olive oil over high heat. Do not move pieces for 2–3 min — allow Maillard crust to form. Turn and colour all faces. Total per batch 6–8 min. Season each batch as it goes in. This step takes 25–30 min total and cannot be rushed.

MIREPOIX: Reduce heat to medium. In the same pot, sweat rough onion, carrot and garlic in remaining fat until soft and lightly coloured, 8–10 min. Add tomato paste, cook out 2–3 min until it darkens and starts to catch. Sift flour over, stir through, cook 2 min to eliminate raw flour taste.

DEGLAZE & BRAISE: Pour in all wine, scrape every bit of fond from the base. Bring to a boil and reduce by half — 15–18 min. Add hot stock, return beef and lardons, add bouquet garni. Liquid should just cover beef. Bring to a simmer, cover tightly, place in oven at 155°C for 2.5–3 hours.

PEARL ONIONS: In a separate wide pan, place onions in a single layer with 20g butter, 15g sugar and enough water to barely cover. Cook uncovered over medium heat until water evaporates and onions glaze to a golden colour, 12–15 min. Season. Reserve.

MUSHROOMS: Heat a wide pan over high heat until smoking. Add 20g butter, add mushrooms in a single layer — do not stir for 2–3 min to allow colour to develop. Season only after colour is achieved — premature salt draws moisture and steams. Toss once, cook 1 more min. Reserve.

SAUCE FINISH: Remove beef with a slotted spoon to a tray. Strain braising liquid through a fine chinois. Return to a wide saucepan, bring to a rolling boil, skim surface fat. Reduce until sauce coats the back of a spoon cleanly. Taste: deep, rounded, slightly tannic. Correct salt.
ASSEMBLE: Return beef chunks to sauce. Fold in glazed onions, mushrooms and lardons gently. Add garnish elements in the final 15 min of holding — not braised back into the sauce.
HOLD: Transfer to deep GN tray. Hold in bain marie at 78–80°C. Stir gently every 20 min.
service: GN EVENT: Ladle portions — approximately 180g beef plus sauce. Ensure each portion captures all three garnish elements. Finish with fine-chopped parsley and picked thyme leaves at the pass.
PLATE SERVICE: Warm wide bowl or lipped plate. Mashed potato or pommes purée as base if offered — 80–100g mash, bourguignon on top, sauce pooled around.
Do not return sauce to a rolling boil with beef in it at service — the beef will tighten and dry. Reheat gently only.
note: Flour quantity is 25g — higher amounts risk a starchy, gluey sauce if not perfectly cooked out. Blanching lardons first is essential — un-blanched smoked speck will oversalt the entire braise. Mushrooms and pearl onions must be cooked and held separately — braising them in the pot produces waterlogged, grey results. Searing in small batches is the single most commonly skipped step — a crowded pan drops temperature, the beef steams, and you get no fond. The fond is the sauce.

name: Creamy Lemon Caper Chicken
subtitle: Pan-Seared Chicken Breast · Lemon Caper Cream Sauce · Herb Finish
label: Lemon Caper Chicken

ingredients.prep: 180–200g each — sinew removed, butterflied or pounded to even 2cm thickness. Even thickness is mandatory for consistent cook across 20 portions.
drained, rinsed if salt-packed — roughly chopped if large, left whole if baby capers
juice only — 90ml approximate. Zest of 1 lemon reserved for finish
fine dice — essential for sauce depth
cold, cubed — 30g for sauce mount, 20g for pan
fine chop — optional, classical pairing with chicken and lemon

method_steps: PREP: Butterfly or pound chicken breasts to an even 2cm thickness between sheets of cling film. Pat completely dry. Season both sides with salt and white pepper immediately before cooking, not ahead — salting early draws moisture to the surface and inhibits the Maillard reaction.

SEAR (in batches): Heat a wide sauté pan over high heat until smoking. Add olive oil. Sear chicken presentation-side down first. Do not move for 3–4 min. Flip, sear 2 min on second side. Remove to a rack on a tray — do not stack.

OVEN FINISH: Transfer seared chicken to a preheated 180°C oven. Cook to 72°C internal temperature measured at the thickest point. Time will be 8–12 min depending on thickness. Remove at 72°C — carryover will reach 74°C during rest. Rest minimum 4 min. Do not cover tightly — steam will soften the sear.

SAUCE — AROMATICS: Without cleaning the sear pan, reduce heat to medium. Add 20g butter. Sweat eschalots until translucent, 3–4 min. Add garlic, cook 1 min — do not allow to colour. Add Dijon mustard, stir through.
SAUCE — DEGLAZE & BUILD: Deglaze with white wine, scraping all fond. Reduce until almost dry — 2–3 min. Add hot chicken stock, bring to a steady simmer, reduce by half, 6–8 min.
SAUCE — CREAM: Add cream. Reduce over medium heat until sauce coats the back of a spoon — approximately 30–35% reduction. Do not boil aggressively once cream is in.

SAUCE — FINISH: Remove pan from heat completely. Add lemon juice (start with 60ml, taste, adjust). Add capers. Mount with cold cubed butter, swirling the pan. Do not return to heat after butter is added. Season with salt and white pepper. Add lemon zest.
HOLD FOR EVENT: Do not add lemon juice or butter until service. Hold cream reduction in a bain marie at 65°C. Finish each GN portion with lemon, butter and capers at the pass.
service: GN EVENT: Slice chicken on an angle into 3–4 pieces. Arrange in tray. Ladle sauce over — 80–90ml per portion. Scatter capers, finish with parsley and lemon zest. Hold at 72°C maximum.
PLATE SERVICE: Pool 80ml sauce in base of warm plate. Fan 3 slices of chicken on sauce. Capers scattered across chicken. Parsley and lemon zest finish.
CRITICAL: Do not hold sauce above 75°C after lemon juice has been added — acid destabilises the cream emulsion. Add lemon juice only at the pass, not in the bain marie.
note: Primary failure mode: sauce breaking in the bain marie after lemon is added. Mitigation: hold cream reduction unseasoned and without lemon; finish each batch portion at the pass with lemon juice, cold butter, and capers. Dijon mustard acts as an emulsifier and stabilises the cream sauce under heat stress. White pepper is specified over black — cream sauces are visually ruined by black specks. Eschalots replace rough garlic-in-pan — provide a sweeter, more refined base appropriate for a cream sauce.

name: Natural Oysters with Champagne Mignonette
subtitle: Live-Shucked Sydney Rock or Pacific · Champagne & Shallot Mignonette · Lemon
label: Natural Oysters

ingredients.prep: live, ordered per-event — 54 to yield 48 good shells after shucking rejects; store cupped-side down on ice in coldroom, do not submerge in water or fresh ice melt
use a bottle open on the day — flat champagne produces a dull mignonette; do not use cooking wine
good quality — not malt or rice wine
fine brunoise, 2 mm — larger pieces dominate the oyster; shallots should be a background note
season mignonette lightly — oysters carry natural salinity
2–3 cracks into mignonette — adds warmth without colour
cut into wedges immediately before service — pre-cut wedges oxidise and dry out
replenish throughout service — ice bed must be level and deep enough to seat shells stably; do not let shells tip and spill liquor

method_steps: OYSTER STORAGE: Receive oysters live — tap any open oyster; it should close. Discard any that do not close. Store cupped-side down on a tray in the coldroom at 4°C. Do not store in standing fresh water or under ice that pools meltwater — fresh water kills oysters. Use a perforated tray with a drip tray beneath.

MIGNONETTE — MAKE AHEAD: Combine champagne, white wine vinegar, fine brunoise shallots, caster sugar, fine salt, and white pepper. Stir to dissolve sugar. Taste — the mignonette should be bright, acidic, slightly sweet, and fragrant. It should complement the oyster's salinity, not compete with it. Cover and refrigerate minimum 1 hour, maximum 4 hours — beyond 4 hours the shallots lose their bite and become sour. Make fresh per event.

SHUCKING: Shuck as close to service as possible — maximum 20 min before passing. Use a proper oyster knife and a folded cloth for grip and hand protection. Insert knife at the hinge, not the lip — lever upward to pop the hinge, then slide knife flat along the top shell to sever the adductor muscle. Keep the cupped shell level to retain the oyster liquor. Run the knife under the oyster in the cup to sever the bottom adductor. Check every shell for fragments — run a clean fingertip around the rim of the meat. Discard any with significant shell loss or off-odour. Arrange on crushed ice immediately.

ICE BED: Build the ice bed deep enough — minimum 40 mm — so each shell sits stably without tipping. A tipping shell loses its liquor; a dry oyster served to a guest is a waste. Level the ice surface before placing shells.
DRESS AT PASS: Drop exactly 5 ml mignonette per oyster using a small squeeze bottle or teaspoon — applied at pass, not on the line. Do not pre-dress more than 8 min before passing.
LEMON: Cut wedges immediately before service. One wedge per 3–4 oysters on the tray.
service: Pass on a deep tray with crushed ice bed. Have a small squeeze bottle of mignonette on the tray for guests who want more — do not rely solely on the self-serve jug; it gets ignored.
Do not pre-dress more than 8 min before pass — mignonette begins to cook the oyster surface and dulls the freshness.

Shucking for 48 oysters: at 1.5 min per oyster for an experienced shucker, allow 75 min total with checks. For a 2-person team where one person shucks while the other passes and replenishes ice, stagger in batches of 12.
Replenish crushed ice throughout service — a warm plate is a rejection waiting to happen.
Alert FOH: guests with shellfish allergies must be identified before the tray leaves the pass.
note: COUNCIL NOTES — The original ingredient quantity listed 48 oysters with no wastage buffer. In professional service, order 10–15% overage — 54 minimum for a 48-piece yield, accounting for rejects, broken shells, and the occasional bad oyster. Fresh water storage kills oysters; this has been clarified in the hold method. Mignonette maximum maceration time added at 4 hours — shallots sour past this point and become acrid. Pre-dress window tightened from 10 min to 8 min. Lemon wedges should be cut immediately before service, not in advance. White pepper added to the mignonette — it is a classical component that adds warmth without visual interruption. This is a simple dish; its quality is entirely dependent on sourcing and timing. Know your fishmonger and confirm delivery day-of.

name: Greek Spiced Roast Lamb Leg
subtitle: Overnight Marinated · Garlic-Studded · Slow-Roasted · Oregano & Lemon Pan Jus
label: Roast Lamb

ingredients.prep: bone-in, ~2.5 kg each — sourced from same supplier batch for even cooking time; remove from coldroom 45 min before roasting to temper
peeled, cut into 6 mm slivers — stud deep into flesh every 5 cm; garlic on the surface burns during uncovered roasting
zest of all 4 into marinade; juice of 3 (approx 90 ml) reserved for deglazing; juice of 1 reserved for finishing at service
good quality — rub into marinade; stale dried oregano contributes nothing
heaped teaspoon — do not substitute cassia; it is sharper and will overwhelm
half a teaspoon — supports the cinnamon, adds warmth depth absent from the original
half a teaspoon — assists colour development and adds mild sweetness
for marinade rub — coat the leg completely
season generously into the marinade and directly into the stud holes — salt must penetrate the flesh, not just sit on the surface
good quality, unsalted — for roasting tray; increases depth of pan jus over plain water
for deglazing after roast — adds acid and lifts fond
reserved from 1 lemon — finish jus off heat at service

method_steps: STUD: Using a small paring knife, pierce the lamb legs all over — deep cuts, 4–5 cm depth, spaced every 5 cm across both legs. Push a garlic sliver into each hole, ensuring it is fully submerged in the flesh. Garlic left proud of the surface will char bitterly during the uncovered roasting phase.

MARINADE: Combine lemon zest from all 4 lemons, dried oregano, ground cinnamon, ground cumin, sweet paprika, olive oil, fine salt, and black pepper. Mix to a paste. Rub the entire surface of both legs thoroughly — under any flap of fat, into the shank end, over the bone knuckle. Place in a deep tray, cover tightly with cling film, and refrigerate overnight — minimum 12 hours, 24 hours optimal. Remove from coldroom 45 min before roasting.

ROASTING TRAY SETUP: Place legs in a deep roasting tray — they should fit without touching. Pour 400 ml stock around, not over, the legs. Cover tightly with two layers of heavy-duty foil, crimped at the edges — any steam escape will dry the meat and stall the braise.

SLOW ROAST — COVERED: Conventional oven at 160°C. Roast covered for 3.5 hours. At the 3-hour mark, probe the thickest part of the leg away from the bone — target 80°C internal. If not yet at 75°C, continue covered for a further 20 min and re-probe.

COLOUR — UNCOVERED: Remove foil. Increase oven to 200°C. Roast uncovered for 20–25 min until skin is deep golden brown and caramelised. Watch closely — the sugars in the lemon zest and paprika will colour fast. Internal temperature target: 88–92°C for pull-apart texture. Do not probe at the bone — bone conducts heat and will give a false high reading.

REST: Remove from oven. Tent loosely with foil — not tight, or steam will soften the crust. Rest minimum 25 min. For a 2.5 kg leg, resting is critical — carving early loses 15–20% of the yield as juice on the board.

PAN JUS: While lamb rests, deglaze roasting tray on the stovetop over high heat with 100 ml white wine. Scrape the fond vigorously — this is where the flavour lives. Add 90 ml reserved lemon juice. Reduce by half. Strain through a fine sieve into a small saucepan. Skim fat from surface. Taste — adjust salt. If thin, reduce further. Finish with 30 ml lemon juice off heat at service for brightness. Jus should be glossy and full-flavoured, not watery.

CARVE: For plated service — carve the leg against the grain into 80–100 g slices. For buffet — pull the meat in chunks, discarding sinew. Arrange on GN tray, ladle jus over, keep covered and hot in the kick oven at 70°C.
HOLD: Carved lamb holds in jus in the kick oven at 70°C for up to 45 min before drying. Do not hold uncovered.
service: Plated service: 2–3 slices or a generous pulled portion per plate, jus spooned at pass — 30–40 ml per portion. Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and fresh oregano leaves.
Buffet: full leg carved tableside for effect, or pulled GN with jus poured over in the kick oven. Label clearly.
For 20 covers from 2 legs: each leg yields approximately 10 plated portions at 100 g served meat. Confirm yield from your supplier's legs before service.
Two-person kitchen: one carves and plates, one ladles jus and garnishes. Do not let the lamb sit plated without jus — it dries immediately.
note: COUNCIL NOTES — Spice profile expanded with ground cumin and sweet paprika: the original recipe was underseasoned for the Greek profile and relied solely on cinnamon, oregano, and lemon. These additions do not overpower; they complete the aromatics. Garlic quantity increased from 8 to 10 cloves and the instruction to stud deep into the flesh has been made explicit — surface garlic burns at 200°C and turns acrid. Stock increased from 300 ml to 400 ml and water replaced with stock — water produces a thin, flat jus; the fond deserves better. White wine deglazing step added — essential for lifting the fond and adding acid structure to the jus. Rest time increased from 20 min to 25 min minimum. Jus finishing with fresh lemon juice off heat specified at 30 ml — this is the brightness that makes the dish sing; without it the jus is heavy and one-dimensional. Internal temperature range corrected to 88–92°C and probe technique (away from bone) specified — bone proximity falsifies the reading. Kick oven hold method added for buffet service: this is a two-person kitchen and the lamb must be managed correctly across a service window.

name: Creamy Potato Gratin
subtitle: Layered · Cream · Gruyère
label: Potato Gratin

ingredients.prep: grated — 150 g in cream mix, 100 g for top

method_steps: CREAM MIX: Combine cream, milk, garlic, nutmeg, salt and white pepper in a saucepan. Bring to a bare simmer over medium heat. Remove from heat — do not boil. Stir in 150 g grated gruyère until melted.

LAYER: Butter a deep gastronorm tray (2/3 GN or equivalent). Layer potatoes in overlapping shingles, seasoning lightly between each layer. Pour hot cream mix over — potatoes should be just submerged. Press down gently.

BAKE: Cover tightly with foil. Bake at 170°C conventional for 60–70 min until potatoes are tender when probed with a skewer. Remove foil, scatter remaining 100 g gruyère over top. Return uncovered for 20–25 min until golden and bubbling.
COOL & PORTION: Rest uncovered for 20 min before portioning. Use a sharp knife and flat spatula for clean rectangular cuts. Portion into 20 equal pieces, approx 150 g each.
service: Portions can be reheated individually in a 180°C oven for 8–10 min or in a bain marie at 85°C.
Hold hot in bain marie covered with lid or foil, max 90 min before quality drops.
Garnish with a small sprig of fresh thyme or chives at pass if plating to plate.
note: Sebago gives better starch binding than Desiree — use Sebago if available. Consistent 3mm slicing is critical for even cook; uneven slices cause underdone layers. Warming the cream mix before pouring helps the top layer cook evenly. Do not skip the rest before portioning — hot gratin will collapse.

name: Garlic & Herb Chat Potatoes with Feta Cream
subtitle: Roasted Chat · Herb Oil · Whipped Feta
label: Chat Potatoes & Feta

ingredients.prep: leaves stripped or whole sprigs for tray
leaves only, fine chop — for herb oil

method_steps: POTATOES: Toss halved chats in olive oil, smashed garlic, rosemary, thyme, salt and pepper. Spread cut-side down on lined roasting trays — do not overcrowd. Roast at 210°C fan-forced (or 220°C conventional) for 30–35 min until deep golden and crisp on the cut face. Do not stir during roasting.

WHIPPED FETA: Blitz feta, cream, lemon juice and zest in food processor until smooth and aerated, approx 90 seconds. Adjust seasoning — note feta is salty, taste before adding salt. Refrigerate until needed; remove 10 min before service.
HERB OIL: Blend parsley leaves, EVOO and microplaned garlic. Season with salt. Pass through a fine sieve for a clean oil, or leave rustic for a sauce consistency. Make same day.

PLATING: Spread whipped feta across base of warm serving platter or bowl. Pile roasted chats over. Drizzle herb oil generously. Finish with a few extra fresh parsley leaves and flaked salt if desired.
service: Best served immediately from oven. If holding, keep potatoes on tray and re-crisp at 220°C for 5 min before plating.
Whipped feta can be made up to 24 hours ahead — store covered in coldroom.
Herb oil made same day; store at room temp in a squeeze bottle for easy service.
note: Danish feta gives a creamier, less grainy result than Greek block feta for the whip. Reduce cream to 80 ml if feta is very wet. Do not crowd roasting trays or potatoes will steam rather than roast — use two trays if needed.

name: Broccoli & Cauliflower Gratin
subtitle: Béchamel · Gruyère · Herb Crumb
label: Veg Gratin

ingredients.prep: florets, blanched 2 min, refreshed in ice water, drained well
florets, blanched 3 min, refreshed in ice water, drained well
grated — 100 g into sauce, 100 g for top

method_steps: BÉCHAMEL: Melt butter in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 min until a pale roux forms. Gradually whisk in warm milk, adding in three additions to avoid lumps. Cook over medium heat, whisking, until thick and smooth, approx 5–7 min. Remove from heat. Stir in 100 g gruyère, dijon, nutmeg. Season well with salt and white pepper. Béchamel should coat the back of a spoon.
CRUMB: Toss panko in olive oil in a dry pan over medium heat. Toast, stirring frequently, for 3–4 min until evenly golden. Remove from heat, cool, then mix with chopped parsley and parmesan. Season.

ASSEMBLE: Arrange blanched, well-drained florets in a buttered gastronorm tray. Pour béchamel over, nudging florets to ensure even coverage. Scatter remaining 100 g gruyère over sauce, then top evenly with herb crumb.
BAKE: Bake at 185°C conventional for 25–30 min until béchamel is bubbling at the edges and crumb is deep golden. If crumb browns too quickly, tent loosely with foil for last 10 min.
HOLD: If prepping ahead, assemble without crumb, refrigerate, and add crumb only in the final 30 min before service bake.
service: Portion into 20 equal serves with a sharp spatula. Serve hot direct from tray.
Holds well in a covered bain marie for up to 60 min — crumb will soften but flavour holds.
For crunch retention, keep tray uncovered under heat lamp rather than in bain marie.
note: Drain florets thoroughly after blanching — excess water will thin the béchamel in the tray. Cauliflower needs an extra minute of blanching over broccoli. Adding parmesan to the crumb improves colour and savouriness. Do not bake raw florets — they will undercook.

name: Prosciutto Wrapped Chicken Breast
subtitle: Prosciutto Wrapped Chicken · Lemon Caper Butter Jus · Herb Finish
label: Prosciutto Chicken

ingredients.prep: 180–200g each — sinew removed. Do NOT butterfly — the whole breast must stay intact for the prosciutto wrap to hold.

ingredients.item: Prosciutto di Parma or San Daniele
ingredients.prep: 2 slices per breast — lay on cling film slightly overlapping to form a rectangle before wrapping. Thin slicing at setting 2.
baby capers, drained and rinsed — left whole
juice of 2 (60ml) for jus; zest of 1 for finish
reduced by half to 250ml before use — weak stock produces a thin jus that will not mount properly
cold, cubed — 60g for jus mount, 20g for basting
fine chop — finish only, not in jus
for inside of wrap only if chicken is very large — prosciutto is heavily salted

method_steps: WRAP (day prior or morning of service): Lay cling film flat. Lay 2 slices prosciutto slightly overlapping to form a 20x15cm rectangle. Season chicken breast with pepper only (no salt — prosciutto provides salt). Place breast at the near edge of prosciutto, tuck in any thin tail to create an even cylinder. Roll tightly using cling film as a guide, twisting ends to compress. Refrigerate wrapped minimum 2 hours, ideally overnight — helps prosciutto adhere.

SEAR: Remove cling film. Heat a wide oven-proof sauté pan over high heat. Add a thin film of olive oil. Place parcels seam-side down first — seals the prosciutto join before any other face. Sear seam-side 2–3 min until prosciutto crisps and grips. Turn carefully and colour remaining faces — total sear time 8–10 min. Prosciutto should be deep golden and slightly crisp on all faces.

ROAST: Transfer pan directly to 185°C oven. Roast 16–20 min. Probe at thickest point — remove at 70°C internal (carryover will take to 74°C during rest). Baste once at the 10-min mark with 20g melted butter. Rest uncovered minimum 5 min on a rack — do not rest flat on a tray or prosciutto bottom steams soft.

JUS — BUILD: Carefully pour off excess fat from searing pan, leaving 1 tsp. Over medium heat, sweat eschalots until soft, 3 min. Add garlic, 1 min. Deglaze with white wine, scraping all fond. Reduce wine to almost dry.
JUS — REDUCE: Add pre-reduced chicken stock (250ml). Bring to a steady simmer, reduce by one-third — 5–8 min — until jus just begins to coat the back of a spoon.

JUS — MOUNT: Remove pan from heat. Add cold butter cubes, swirling constantly to create an emulsified, glossy jus. Do not return to heat after butter is added. Add lemon juice (start with 40ml, taste), capers, and lemon zest. Season with pepper.

HOLD (for event): Rest and hold whole wrapped breasts on a rack in a low oven at 65°C, uncovered. Jus held separately in a bain marie at 60°C, butter not yet added. Mount jus with butter and finish with lemon and capers at the pass.
SERVICE CUT: Slice each breast on an angle in half or into 3 pieces. If prosciutto has gone soggy during hold, run under a salamander for 60 sec before service.
service: PLATE: 1 breast per portion. Place chicken slightly off-centre. Spoon or pour 40–50ml jus around base of chicken — not over the top. The prosciutto crust is the visual and textural hero; do not drown it in sauce.
GARNISH: Fine parsley and lemon zest over the chicken.
GN EVENT: Hold whole breasts as above. Slice to order at the pass if possible. Jus in squeeze bottle for controlled portioning.
CRISP FACTOR: If prosciutto has softened, a 45-second pass under a hot salamander before plating restores crispness.
note: Preferred wrap method is the cling film roll-and-refrigerate technique — results in a self-adhering prosciutto shell without toothpicks (foreign body contamination risk at 80 covers). Stock pre-reduced to 250ml gives the jus necessary concentration before butter mount. Seam-side searing first is the correct sequence — if you sear presentation-side first, the seam opens under heat. Prosciutto chicken must rest on a rack, never flat — steam from the tray makes the underside soft. High oven temperature (185°C) is deliberate — at 180°C the prosciutto risks going leathery rather than crisp.

name: Fillet of Beef with Tempranillo Reduction
subtitle: Eye Fillet · Tempranillo & Demi-Glace Jus · Broccolini
label: Beef Fillet

ingredients.prep: trimmed of all silverskin and chain — do not remove all surface fat but remove sinew. Portion into 180–200g steaks cut from the centre and upper tail. Tie with butcher's twine to maintain cylinder shape if steaks are from the wider section.
drinking quality, dry — Grenache-Tempranillo blend acceptable. Do not use cheap cooking wine; at this reduction ratio the flaws concentrate.
quality commercial or house-made — must be glossy and fully gelatinised at room temperature. If thin or liquid, reduce separately before use.
1 bunch per 4 portions approximate — trimmed, 2cm of base removed
flaky salt for beef crust specifically
optional acid correction for jus at finish

method_steps: JUS — MAKE AHEAD (day before or morning of service): Sweat eschalots in a small saucepan over medium heat until soft and translucent, 4–5 min. Add cracked peppercorns, thyme and bay leaf. Add Tempranillo, bring to a steady boil, reduce by two-thirds — 20–25 min. The wine must fully reduce or it carries a raw, tannic harshness into the finished jus. Add demi-glace. Reduce over medium heat until jus coats the back of a spoon with a clean line and is slightly syrupy. Strain through a fine chinois. Refrigerate. The jus should set to a loose gel when cold — if it does not, your demi was too weak.

JUS — SERVICE FINISH: Reheat strained base to a gentle simmer. Do not boil aggressively. Remove from heat, mount with cold butter cubes by swirling or whisking in. The jus should be glossy, lightly coating, deeply coloured. Hold in a bain marie at 65°C with cling film on the surface to prevent skinning. Mount with butter only at service time.

BEEF — TEMPER: Remove portioned fillets from coldroom 40–45 min before service. Place on a rack at room temperature. Season generously with salt flakes and cracked pepper on all faces immediately before searing — not earlier.

BEEF — SEAR: Heat a cast iron or heavy-base pan over the highest available heat until it begins to smoke lightly. Add olive oil. Sear fillets in batches of 4–5 maximum. Each fillet needs 2–3 min per side and a brief sear on the rounded edges — total 8–10 min per batch. Between batches, wipe the pan of charred debris with a folded cloth, reheat, add fresh oil.

BEEF — OVEN: Transfer all seared fillets to a wire rack set over a tray. Place in oven at 200°C (not fan-forced). Probe target: remove at 52°C for medium-rare service. Carryover during rest will take to 55–57°C. Time: 8–12 min depending on thickness. Do not rely on time alone — use a probe on every batch.
BEEF — REST: Remove from oven, tent loosely with foil — not sealed tight. Rest minimum 8 min, ideally 10 min.

BROCCOLINI: Blanch in heavily salted boiling water for 90 seconds. Refresh immediately in iced water. Drain and dry on paper. At service: heat a wide pan over high heat, add olive oil and garlic slices — cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not coloured. Add broccolini, toss to coat and heat through 2 min. Add butter, season, toss once more. Serve immediately.
service: PLATE TO ORDER: This dish must be plated at the pass, not held in a GN. Eye fillet at medium-rare is not a bain-marie dish. Two-person team: one person slicing and placing beef, one person saucing and adding broccolini.

PLATE BUILD: Warm plate. Broccolini placed first as a base or to one side. Fillet (whole or sliced on the bias into 2–3 pieces) placed on or alongside. Jus spooned or poured table-side from a small warm jug — 40–50ml per portion. Salt flakes on the cut face of the beef only.

TIMING AT 80 COVERS: Sear beef in batches from 30 min before service start. Do not put all 20 portions in oven simultaneously — a loaded oven drops temperature. Preheat to 210°C and allow for recovery between batches.
REST HOLD: Seared-and-rested fillets can hold at 58°C in a low oven for up to 15 min before quality drop.
note: Probe every fillet individually — 52°C out of oven, 55–57°C at plate. Rest is non-negotiable — 8 min minimum on a rack, not a flat tray. Jus must be mounted with butter off heat only — a broken jus on eye fillet at an event is a presentation failure. Tempranillo is the correct wine choice — dried-fruit and earthy tannin that complements beef without the jammy sweetness of Shiraz. Red wine vinegar (5ml) is flagged as optional acid correction at finish — add drop by drop if jus reads sweet-heavy.

name: Stuffed Mushroom with Romesco & Lemon Aioli
subtitle: Roasted Cup Mushroom · Parmesan & Herb Filling · Romesco · Lemon Aioli
label: Stuffed Mushroom

ingredients.prep: stalks removed, wiped clean with damp cloth — do not wash
fine chop, squeezed dry in cloth to remove moisture
finely grated — extra, for gratinating tops

method_steps: MUSHROOMS: Preheat oven to 200°C (fan-forced). Toss mushrooms in olive oil, season with sea salt and white pepper. Arrange gill-side up on lined trays — do not crowd. Roast 12–14 min until gill edges colour and moisture begins to release. Tip each mushroom briefly to drain pooled liquid — excess water will waterlog the filling and collapse it.

FILLING: While mushrooms roast, beat cream cheese until smooth. Fold in parmesan, microplaned garlic, lemon zest, and parsley. Season assertively — filling must be seasoned enough to carry through the mushroom. Taste on a cracker to calibrate, not off the spoon.
FILL: Transfer filling to piping bag fitted with a plain 14mm tip. Pipe a generous dome into each mushroom cap. Alternatively spoon and smooth. Dust tops with reserved grated parmesan.

GRATINATE: Return filled mushrooms to 200°C oven for 6–7 min until filling is set, parmesan top is golden, and edges show light caramelisation. Do not undercook — cold filling centre is the single most common failure point on this canape.

SAUCE PREP: Spoon romesco into a small squeezy bottle and lemon aioli into a second. Both should be fluid enough to dot cleanly — thin with a few drops of water if batch-made sauce has thickened in the coldroom.
SERVE: Transfer to pass. Dot three points of romesco and two points of lemon aioli directly onto each mushroom or around the base on the serving piece. Finish with a pinch of parsley.
service: Serve warm, minimum 65°C at pass. Two sauces give colour contrast — alternate placement so both are visible.

Prep-ahead window: roast mushrooms and make filling up to 4 hours ahead. Store separately chilled. Fill and gratinate to order in batches of 20. Do not pre-fill and hold raw — the filling absorbs mushroom moisture and goes slack.
Two-person service: one person fills and loads trays, second person sauces and passes. Cycle through kick oven in 8-min windows for continuous canape flow.
Flag for front-of-house: these do not hold on a tray — 10-minute window maximum before the filling weeps and the base softens.
note: The critical failure mode is waterlogged mushrooms. Cup mushrooms release significant moisture — the pre-drain step after initial roasting is non-negotiable. Skipping it produces a soggy base and filling that slides. Parsley moisture is a secondary issue — wet parsley turns the filling grey within 30 minutes; squeeze it dry before folding in. Lemon zest in the filling replaces the acid balance that the raw garlic would otherwise dominate. Filling seasoning must be calibrated before piping — once inside the mushroom, you cannot correct it. Vegan option: replace cream cheese with whipped cashew cream (soaked 4 hrs, blended with 15 ml lemon juice and 5 g nutritional yeast per 200 g) and parmesan with 30 g nutritional yeast — filling will be softer, reduce lemon zest to avoid curdling.

name: Roving Cannoli & Tiramisu Service
subtitle: Passed Dessert · Cannoli · Individual Tiramisu Cups
label: Roving Desserts

ingredients.prep: drained overnight in fridge through muslin or fine sieve

ingredients.item: Shot Cups or 80 ml Espresso Glasses — Tiramisu

method_steps: CANNOLI FILLING: Combine drained ricotta, icing sugar, cooled white chocolate and a pinch of salt. Mix until smooth — do not over-beat or it becomes loose. Taste and adjust sweetness. Transfer to a piping bag with a plain 10 mm nozzle. Refrigerate until needed. Fill shells from both ends at the last possible moment before passing.

ZABAGLIONE: Whisk egg yolks and caster sugar in a bowl over a bain marie (water at a bare simmer, bowl not touching water). Whisk continuously for 6–8 min until mixture is pale, thick, and holds a ribbon trail. Remove from heat and whisk in a stand mixer or by hand until completely cool, approx 5 min.

TIRAMISU CREAM: Fold cooled zabaglione into mascarpone until combined and smooth. Gently fold in whipped cream in two additions — do not overmix or the cream will deflate. Mixture should be airy but hold shape.
ESPRESSO MIX: Combine cooled espresso and marsala in a shallow bowl.

ASSEMBLE CUPS: Briefly dip savoiardi pieces in espresso-marsala mix for 2 seconds each side — do not soak or they become waterlogged. Place a layer in base of each cup. Pipe or spoon tiramisu cream to fill. Add a second savoiardi layer if cup allows, then top with cream. Level surface with small palette knife. Refrigerate a minimum of 3 hours, ideally overnight.
DUST: Pass Dutch cocoa through a small fine sieve directly over cups at service only.
service: Cannoli: fill shells at the pass using piping bag — maximum 5 min before eating. Press cut pistachio into both open ends after filling. Pass on a lined tray.
Tiramisu cups: pre-set on trays in coldroom. Remove 10 min before passing. Dust cocoa with a small sieve at pass only — not in advance.
Stagger cannoli and tiramisu passes across service so both remain fresh.
note: Ricotta must be well drained or cannoli filling will be too loose to pipe cleanly. Zabaglione must be fully cooled before folding into mascarpone or the cream will split. Cannoli shells absorb moisture quickly — keep unfilled until the final moment. Tiramisu cups can be made day prior for best set.

name: House Baked Scones Platter
subtitle: Warm Scones · Chantilly · House Jam
label: Scones Platter

ingredients.prep: cold — plus a splash for brushing tops

ingredients.item: Strawberry Jam — house-made or quality commercial

method_steps: SCONES: Combine flour, caster sugar and salt in a large bowl. Add cold cubed butter and rub into flour using fingertips until mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs with some pea-sized pieces remaining — do not over-rub.

MIX: Make a well in the centre. Add cold buttermilk all at once. Use a butter knife or pastry scraper to bring dough together with a cutting action — mix until just combined. Dough will look shaggy. Do not knead.

SHAPE: Turn onto a lightly floured bench. Pat gently to 3 cm thick — handle as little as possible. Use a sharp 5 cm round cutter dipped in flour. Press straight down and lift cleanly — do not twist. Re-pat scraps once only.

BAKE: Place on a lined tray with scones touching (encourages rise). Brush tops with buttermilk. Bake at 220°C conventional for 12–14 min until risen, golden on top, and cooked through. Cool on a wire rack for 5 min before serving.
CHANTILLY: Whip cold cream, icing sugar and vanilla in a chilled bowl to soft-firm peaks. Peaks should hold shape but not be stiff. Refrigerate in a covered container until service.
PLATTER ASSEMBLY: Halve scones and arrange cut-side up on platter. Place Chantilly in a small bowl and jam in a ramekin alongside.
service: Serve warm. If made ahead, reheat scones at 160°C for 5–6 min — do not overheat or they dry out.
Chantilly in a small bowl or piping bag on platter. Jam in a ramekin with a small spoon.
Do not pre-split scones more than 15 min before service — cut surfaces dry quickly.
note: Overworking the dough is the primary failure point — the less handling after liquid is added, the better the rise. Cold butter and cold buttermilk are essential. Scones touching on the tray help them rise straighter. Yield is approximately 40 x 40 g scones from this batch.

name: Sweet Slice & Cake Platter
subtitle: Mixed Slices · Mini Cakes · House Selection
label: Sweet Platter

ingredients.prep: baked day prior, fully set, portioned into 4 cm squares

ingredients.item: Lemon Slice or Lemon Tart — House Recipe
ingredients.prep: baked same day or day prior, portioned to match
house-baked or quality supplier, day of event
garnish — rinse and dry thoroughly before service

method_steps: CARAMEL SLICE: Bake the day before the event — the caramel layer requires a full overnight set in the coldroom before clean portioning. Portion with a warm dry knife into 4 cm squares. Hold portioned pieces on a lined tray covered in the coldroom.
LEMON SLICE: Bake same day or day prior. Portion to consistent size matching caramel slice pieces. Keep chilled until 20 min before service.
CUPCAKES: Bake day of event or morning of. Ice or finish as per house recipe. Hold at room temperature if event is within 4 hours of baking.

ASSEMBLE: Bring all pieces to room temperature 15–20 min before service. Arrange on platter alternating colours, heights and types for visual balance. Keep heavier items flat, lighter garnishes on top.
FINISH: Dust icing sugar lightly over the platter just before service. Place berries or edible flowers across platter at the last moment.
service: Hold assembled platter covered loosely with cling film or a platter cover until service — slices dry out quickly once exposed.
Dust icing sugar and add fresh berries or edible flowers at the pass only.
Room temperature service. Do not refrigerate assembled platter — condensation affects caramel slice coating.
note: Selection should vary by season and availability. Always include at least one gluten-free option where possible — label clearly. Caramel slice must be fully cold-set before cutting or the caramel layer will smear. A warm knife helps clean cuts through chocolate-topped slices.

name: Grazing Box
subtitle: Cheese · Charcuterie · Crackers · Fruit · Dips
label: Grazing Box

ingredients.prep: whole or halved to show interior
partially sliced, partially broken into shards
in a small jar or ramekin set in the box
honeycomb in a corner or honey in small jar

method_steps: TEMP: Remove cheeses from coldroom 30–40 min before service. Charcuterie can come out 15 min before — do not temper in a warm kitchen.
ASSEMBLE — ANCHOR: Place dip ramekin and cheeses first at different corners and intervals to anchor the layout. Brie whole or halved, cheddar partially pre-sliced for accessibility.
BUILD: Arrange charcuterie in loose folds and rolls adjacent to cheeses. Fan or stack crackers in clear sections. Tuck dried fruits and nuts into gaps. Add fresh fruit clusters.
FINISH: Position honeycomb or honey jar. Tuck in fresh herb sprigs. Add cornichons or pickles if using — place near charcuterie for balance.
COVER: Loosely cover with cling film and refrigerate if not delivering immediately. Remove film just before delivery.
service: Deliver and present at room temperature. Do not refrigerate the assembled box once at the venue.
Include cheese knives (one per cheese type), small plates, and napkins with the box.
If delivery time exceeds 30 min in warm conditions, transport cheeses and charcuterie separately in a chilled bag and assemble on arrival.
note: Always include at least one soft, one semi-hard, and one hard cheese for variety. Scale all quantities proportionally for larger boxes. Adding a small acid element (cornichons, pickles) lifts the overall balance against the richness of cheese and cured meats. Crackers should be stored separately and added at assembly or delivery to prevent sogginess.

name: Ham & Cheese Croissants Platter
subtitle: Leg Ham · Gruyère · Dijon Butter · Warm
label: Ham Croissants

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced or grated — approx 7 g per croissant

method_steps: DIJON BUTTER: Beat softened butter and dijon together until fully combined. Season with a pinch of salt and white pepper. Transfer to a small container. Can be made 48 hours ahead — hold in coldroom.
FILL: Split croissants along the top without cutting fully through. Spread dijon butter generously on both inner faces — approx 3 g per side. Layer ham, then cheese. Press gently to close.
TRAY: Arrange filled croissants on lined baking trays, seam-side down, spaced apart. Can be filled up to 2 hours ahead and held covered in coldroom.
BAKE: Bake at 170°C conventional for 8–10 min until cheese is melted, ham is warmed through, and croissant exterior is hot and slightly crisped. Do not overbake or croissants dry out.
SERVE: Transfer immediately to lined platter. Cover loosely with a linen cloth or foil tent to hold heat during pass.
service: Serve hot. Cover platter with a folded linen until actively passing.
Batch-warm in waves of 20 if total count is high — stagger trays 8–10 min apart to maintain fresh-baked quality through the pass.
Do not hold in bain marie — steam softens the croissant exterior.
note: Gruyère melts cleanly and is the preferred cheese for this application. Avoid processed slices. Dijon butter can be scaled and made in advance for the week. If using large croissants instead of mini, adjust to 20 pieces and increase ham to 40 g per croissant.

name: Sandwich Platter
subtitle: Mixed Fillings · White & Wholemeal · Finger Sandwiches
label: Sandwich Platter

ingredients.prep: poached in salted water, cooled, thinly sliced or pulled
dry — do not use wet leaves

method_steps: FILLING 1 — CHICKEN MAYO: Combine poached chicken, mayonnaise and lemon juice. Season well with salt and pepper. Mixture should be generously bound but not wet.
FILLING 2 — ROAST BEEF: Mix whole grain mustard with a small amount of horseradish cream if using. Spread on bread; layer roast beef over.
FILLING 3 — CHEDDAR & SPINACH: Layer cheddar slices with dry baby spinach. Season.
ASSEMBLE: Butter all bread slices generously to the edges — this seals moisture in and prevents sogging. Apply fillings evenly, right to the edges. Press sandwiches firmly together.
TRIM & CUT: Remove crusts with a sharp serrated knife using a single clean stroke per side. Cut into fingers (3 per sandwich) or triangles. Do not saw.
HOLD: Stack neatly, wrap tightly in cling film by filling type, label, and refrigerate immediately. Use within 4 hours of assembly.
service: Unwrap and arrange on platter at service, alternating bread types and fillings for colour.
Cover platter with a lightly dampened cloth (not wet) if holding longer than 20 min before passing to prevent edges drying.
Label dietary options clearly on the platter, especially chicken and beef.
note: At least 3 distinct fillings per platter. Butter to the edges is essential — it acts as a moisture barrier. Wet fillings (chicken mayo) should not be applied too thick or bread will sog. Spinach must be completely dry. If making the day prior, wrap individually and store in coldroom — quality degrades after 18 hours.

name: Premium Filled Wraps Platter
subtitle: Grilled Chicken · Prawn & Slaw · Roast Veg · Assorted Wraps
label: Wraps Platter

ingredients.prep: room temp — cold tortillas crack when rolling
seasoned, grilled at 220°C grill plate, rested, sliced 5 mm

ingredients.item: Roasted Capsicum, Zucchini and Red Onion
ingredients.prep: tossed in olive oil, roasted at 200°C until caramelised, cooled

method_steps: SLAW: Toss finely shredded cabbage with apple cider vinegar, caster sugar, salt and pepper. Rest 15 min, then drain off excess liquid before using — wet slaw soaks through wraps.
CHICKEN WRAP FILLING: Combine sliced grilled chicken with 80 ml mayonnaise. Season well.
PRAWN WRAP FILLING: Mix prawns with sriracha, remaining mayonnaise and lemon juice. Season.
VEG WRAP FILLING: Combine roasted veg with a small amount of mayo if desired. Season and cool completely before filling.

ROLL: Lay tortilla flat. Spread a thin layer of mayo or filling sauce across the lower two-thirds. Layer fillings horizontally across the lower third — do not overfill. Fold in sides, then roll tightly away from you. Roll should be firm with no air gaps.
WRAP & CHILL: Wrap each roll tightly in cling film, twisting ends. Refrigerate seam-side down for minimum 30 min to set shape.
CUT: Remove cling film. Slice diagonally into thirds with a sharp knife in a single downward stroke. Arrange cut-side up on platter.
service: Display cut-side up to show fillings — arrange by type in rows or clusters for ease of identification.
Hold chilled on trays covered with cling film until service. Unwrap and plate at the last moment.
Label each wrap type on the platter. Prawn wraps must be clearly identified for allergen awareness.
note: Cold tortillas will crack — bring to room temp before rolling. Drain slaw thoroughly before use or wraps will become soggy within 30 min. Do not overfill — the most common cause of wraps unrolling on the platter. Slice only at service for best cross-section presentation. Prawn wraps are an allergen risk — ensure separate handling and clear labelling.

name: Filled Brioche Rolls Platter
subtitle: Prawn Cocktail · Smoked Salmon · Chicken Caesar · Brioche
label: Brioche Rolls Platter

ingredients.prep: thin sliced, cut to fit roll
fine dice or pulled — no large chunks
fine shred — base layer for chicken rolls

method_steps: COCKTAIL SAUCE: Combine mayonnaise, tomato sauce, lemon juice and Tabasco. Whisk smooth. Season with salt and white pepper. Chill until needed.
SALMON CREAM: Beat softened cream cheese until smooth. Fold in lemon juice and dill. Season. Transfer to piping bag — will pipe cleaner than spreading.
CAESAR CHICKEN: Combine diced chicken and caesar dressing. Fold in parmesan. Season. Mix should be lightly bound, not wet — add dressing cautiously.
ROLLS: Lightly toast cut-side of brioche rolls on flat grill or in oven at 180°C for 3–4 min until just golden. Cool before filling.
FILL — PRAWN: Spoon cocktail sauce onto base. Arrange 2–3 prawn halves cut-side up. Close.
FILL — SALMON: Pipe cream cheese mix onto base. Lay 1–2 slices smoked salmon folded. Close.
FILL — CAESAR: Place small shred of cos on base. Spoon chicken caesar mix. Close.
HOLD: Arrange filled rolls on lined trays in groups by filling type. Cover with damp cloth then cling wrap. Refrigerate at 4°C or below. Maximum hold 2 hours before service.
service: Arrange on platter in three distinct rows or clusters by filling type — prawn, salmon, chicken.
Place small label card or toothpick flag at each group to identify filling.
Remove cling wrap at last moment. Do not plate more than 20 min before guests arrive.
Keep platter on ice bed or chilled surface if ambient temp exceeds 22°C.
note: Toast cut-side of rolls — prevents sogginess from fillings. Salmon cream piping bag speeds service significantly for large runs. Caesar chicken should be lightly dressed — overdressing makes rolls wet and heavy. Flag: no Kuschi exclusions present.

name: Roast Beef & Beetroot Balsamic Platter
subtitle: Rare Roast Beef · Beetroot Relish · Horseradish Cream · Crostini
label: Roast Beef Platter

ingredients.prep: sliced 8–10 mm, brushed with olive oil, toasted
sliced 2–3 mm — ask butcher or slice on deli slicer

method_steps: CROSTINI: Preheat oven to 190°C conventional. Lay sliced bread on lined trays. Brush tops with olive oil. Bake 8–10 min until golden and crisp. Transfer to rack. Cool completely before assembling — warm crostini will steam and soften under toppings.

HORSERADISH CREAM: Whip thickened cream to soft peaks. Drain horseradish well, squeezing out liquid in a cloth or fine sieve — excess liquid will break the cream. Fold horseradish into cream with red wine vinegar. Season with salt and pepper. Taste — should be clearly pungent. Adjust horseradish if mild. Chill until needed.
BEEF: Slice to 2–3 mm if not pre-sliced. Keep covered and chilled. Remove from fridge 15 min before service so it doesn't crack when draped.

ASSEMBLE TO ORDER: Spread 1 tsp horseradish cream on each crostini. Drape 1–2 slices rare beef folded loosely. Dot 1 tsp beetroot relish to one side. Scatter chives. Finish with a flake of salt and crack of pepper.
service: Assemble maximum 15 min before service — beyond this crostini soften from horseradish cream moisture.
Display on slate or dark board for colour contrast.
Do not stack — keeps crostini crisp and presentation clean.
If holding beyond 15 min, keep components separate and assemble in two passes.
note: Horseradish pungency varies by brand — taste and adjust before service. Squeeze excess liquid from prepared horseradish thoroughly or cream will not hold. Roast beef should be clearly rare to medium-rare — grey beef reads as poor quality at canape level. Flag: no Kuschi exclusions present.

name: Dietary Grazing Platter
subtitle: GF · Vegan · Dairy-Free · Nut-Optional
label: Dietary Platter

ingredients.item: GF Rice Crackers or Corn Thins

ingredients.prep: batons 8 cm x 8 mm uniform cut
batons 8 cm x 8 mm, strings removed
unsalted, raw or dry-roasted — see nut allergy note

ingredients.item: Fresh Herb Sprigs or Edible Flowers

method_steps: DIPS: Portion hummus and white bean dip into individual ramekins or small bowls (minimum 80 g per bowl). Drizzle each with EVOO, dust with paprika or za'atar. Do not combine into one bowl — keeps dietary integrity clear.
CRUDITÉS: Trim and cut carrots and celery to uniform 8 cm x 8 mm batons. Store in iced water in coldroom until 15 min before service, then drain and pat dry.
FRUIT: Wash and prep strawberries and grapes. Keep refrigerated until assembly.
BOARD BUILD: Place ramekins first to anchor board layout. Build crudités in upright clusters or fanned rows. Fill sections with crackers, fruit clusters, nut/dried fruit mix. Add herb garnish last.
LABEL: Place small cards identifying each dip by name and dietary status (GF, Vegan, Dairy-Free) directly in front of ramekin.
service: Serve at room temperature — remove from coldroom 10 min before service.
Label each dip clearly: name, GF, Vegan, Dairy-Free as applicable.
Board must not share surface with any non-vegan or gluten-containing items — use a dedicated board.
If nut allergy flagged for the group, replace mixed nuts with 80 g pepitas or sunflower seeds — same volume and visual effect.
note: Babaganoush is eggplant-based and falls under Kuschi's exclusion list — substitute with white bean dip or roasted capsicum dip. Confirm any purchased hummus or dip is certified GF and vegan — not just labelled 'vegetarian'. Sulphite-free dried fruit recommended for allergy-sensitive groups. Nut substitution with seeds is a straight swap — prep same way.

name: Savoury Filled Croissants
subtitle: Ham & Gruyère · Smoked Salmon & Cream Cheese
label: Savoury Croissants

ingredients.prep: split three-quarters through, not fully separated
thin sliced — approx. 15–20 g per croissant
fine slice, garnish for salmon croissants

method_steps: CREAM CHEESE MIX: Beat softened cream cheese until smooth. Fold in lemon juice, dill and capers. Season with salt and white pepper. Chill. Transfer to piping bag for service.
HAM & GRUYÈRE: Fold 2–3 slices ham and lay inside croissant with a layer of gruyère on top. Do not overfill — croissant should close. Place on lined baking tray.

WARM: Bake ham and gruyère croissants at 170°C fan-forced for 8–10 min until cheese is melted and croissant is warmed through. Core temp should reach 65°C minimum. Do not overbake — croissant will dry out.
SALMON: Pipe cream cheese mix generously onto base of croissant. Lay 15–20 g smoked salmon folded on top. Scatter chives. Close. Do NOT warm — assemble cold and hold refrigerated.
HOLD: Ham croissants — hold in warming tray at 60°C max for no more than 20 min. Salmon croissants — covered tray in coldroom at 4°C until service.
service: Transfer ham croissants to platter immediately before service — hot from oven or warming tray.
Salmon croissants come direct from coldroom to platter — do not allow to sit at room temp more than 20 min.
Use two separate platters clearly labelled: Ham & Gruyère and Smoked Salmon.
Replenish in small batches rather than holding full batch at service temperature.
note: Never warm smoked salmon — it cooks, loses texture and the flavour deteriorates. Maintain strict separation between warm and cold platters. Cream cheese mix can be made night before and held in piping bag. If gruyère unavailable, Swiss or Emmental are acceptable substitutes.

name: Sweet Filled Croissants
subtitle: Chantilly & Strawberry · Nutella & Banana · Almond Frangipane
label: Sweet Croissants

ingredients.prep: sliced 5 mm on bias — assemble last
toss with banana to delay browning

method_steps: CHANTILLY: Combine cold thickened cream, icing sugar and vanilla in a chilled bowl. Whip to soft-firm peaks — cream should hold shape but not be grainy. Transfer to piping bag with star tip. Refrigerate until needed.

ALMOND CROISSANTS: Pipe or spread 15–20 g frangipane inside each croissant. Top with toasted flaked almonds. Place on lined baking tray. Bake at 170°C fan-forced for 8–10 min until frangipane is set and croissant is warmed through. Cool on rack 5 min before plating.
CHANTILLY & STRAWBERRY: Pipe chantilly generously along base of croissant. Lay 4–5 strawberry slices overlapping on cream. Close. Assemble no more than 30 min before service — cream softens quickly.

NUTELLA & BANANA: Toss banana slices in lemon juice immediately after cutting. Spread 15 g Nutella on base of croissant. Lay banana slices. Close. Assemble last of the three varieties — banana discolours fastest.
DUST: Dust all assembled croissants lightly with icing sugar immediately before plating.
service: Almond croissants served warm — plate first and replenish from oven as needed.
Chantilly and Nutella croissants at room temperature.
Assemble Nutella & banana croissants last, no more than 20 min before service.
Mixed platter with varieties visible — do not stack. Label each variety.
Icing sugar dusted at last moment — absorbs quickly on cream-filled croissants.
note: Lemon juice toss on banana slows browning by 30–40 min — critical for batches. Chantilly croissants are the most time-sensitive; prioritise assembling these closest to service. Frangipane from bakery supplier is consistent and saves prep time for a 2-person team. Almond croissants can be baked 30 min ahead and held at room temp — they hold better than cream-filled varieties.

name: Mixed Bircher Muesli Bowls
subtitle: Rolled Oats · Apple · Yoghurt · Berry Compote · Toasted Seeds
label: Bircher Muesli

ingredients.prep: plus extra 100–150 ml for morning loosen
plus extra 100 g for morning loosen
grated on coarse side, skin on — squeeze out excess juice lightly

ingredients.item: Mixed Berry Compote — house or quality

ingredients.prep: sliced to order — mango, banana, kiwi or stone fruit

method_steps: BIRCHER BASE: Combine oats, 500 ml apple juice, 400 g yoghurt, grated apple, honey, cinnamon and cardamom in a large container. Stir thoroughly to combine — all oats should be moistened. Cover and refrigerate overnight (minimum 8 hours, maximum 24 hours).
SEEDS: Toast pepitas or sunflower seeds on a dry tray at 170°C for 8 min, tossing halfway, until golden and fragrant. Cool completely. Store in airtight container.

MORNING PREP: Remove bircher from coldroom. Mixture will have thickened considerably. Add 100–150 ml apple juice and 100 g yoghurt gradually, stirring to loosen to a thick porridge consistency — should drop slowly from a spoon, not set solid.
PORTION: Portion 150–160 g bircher per bowl (ladle or portion scoop). Cover with cling wrap. Return to coldroom at 4°C until service. Do not add toppings until service.
SERVICE SETUP: Set up compote, fresh fruit and seeds in separate containers at the pass for rapid assembly.
service: Remove portioned bowls from coldroom no more than 10 min before service.
At service: spoon 30 g berry compote over bircher, arrange fresh fruit, scatter 5–6 g seeds, drizzle honey to order.
Keep compote and fresh fruit chilled in coldroom containers — spoon to order rather than pre-topping all bowls.
Label bowls if dairy-free alternative yoghurt used for specific guests.
note: Bircher must be made the night before — minimum 8-hour soak for oats to fully hydrate. Always loosen with additional liquid before portioning or texture will be too dense. Grated apple adds natural sweetness and moisture — do not omit. Cardamom is a small addition that lifts the flavour profile significantly. Toasted seeds must be fully cooled before service or they will create condensation and soften.

name: Chicken Caesar Salad Bowl
subtitle: Grilled Chicken Thigh · Cos · Caesar Dressing · Parmesan · Soft Egg
label: Caesar Bowl

ingredients.prep: outer leaves removed, inner leaves torn to 5–6 cm pieces, washed and dried

method_steps: MARINADE: Combine olive oil, microplaned garlic, lemon juice and salt. Coat chicken thighs. Marinate minimum 30 min refrigerated, maximum overnight.

EGGS: Bring water to a rolling boil. Lower eggs gently. Cook 7 min exactly. Transfer to iced water for 3 min. Peel carefully. Halve at service. Store peeled and whole in coldroom covered with damp cloth.

CHICKEN: Grill on preheated char grill or flat grill at medium-high heat, 5–6 min each side. Internal temperature must reach 74°C at thickest point — check with probe. Rest 5 min before slicing on the bias to 5–6 mm slices.
SALAD BASE: Toss torn cos leaves with 150 ml caesar dressing — dress lightly, leaves should glisten not be saturated. Season. Reserve remaining 100 ml dressing for service.
BOWL BUILD: Base of dressed cos. Fan 4–5 slices chicken on one side. Shaved parmesan over. Half egg placed cut-side up. Croutons added last on top. Parsley scatter. Crack of pepper.
DRESSING SERVICE: Remaining dressing in a pourer or small jug on the side.
service: Dress salad base to order or in small batches of 5 portions maximum — pre-dressed cos wilts within 10 min.
Croutons go on last — even 5 min in dressed salad will soften them.
Dressing in jug on side for self-service or top-up.
Chicken can be grilled up to 1 hour ahead, rested, sliced and held at 60°C in bain marie. Do not slice until service if holding more than 20 min.
note: Probe chicken to 74°C — thighs are forgiving but must reach safe temp. Soft boiled eggs are a key textural element — 7 min gives a set white and jammy yolk. If egg preference is unknown for the group, confirm ahead or offer on side. Do not pre-dress bowls for a buffet run — they deteriorate fast. Flag: no Kuschi exclusions present.

name: Lamb Fattoush Salad Bowl
subtitle: Spiced Lamb · Fattoush · Pomegranate · Labneh · Sumac
label: Lamb Fattoush

ingredients.item: Lamb Mince or Diced Lamb Leg
ingredients.prep: if diced, cut to 2 cm cubes
leaves and tender stems, rough chop
leaves only, kept whole or torn

method_steps: LAMB SPICE MIX: Combine cumin, coriander, cinnamon, paprika and salt. Coat lamb thoroughly. Rest 15 min at room temp before cooking.

COOK LAMB: Heat a heavy-based pan or flat grill to high. Add olive oil. Cook lamb in batches — do not crowd the pan. For mince: cook 5–6 min, stirring to caramelise and break up. For diced leg: sear 2–3 min each side until well-coloured, then finish in oven at 200°C for 5–6 min to 70°C internal. Rest 5 min. Break mince into rough chunks rather than fine crumble for texture.

FLATBREAD: Toss torn flatbread pieces in olive oil. Spread on lined tray. Bake at 190°C fan-forced for 8–10 min until golden and crisp. Watch closely — they burn quickly. Cool on rack. Add to salad only at service.
FATTOUSH DRESSING: Whisk lemon juice, EVOO and 5 g sumac together. Season.
FATTOUSH BASE: Combine cos, parsley, mint and spring onion in a large bowl. Do not dress until service.

BOWL BUILD: Toss fattoush base with dressing at service. Base of dressed fattoush in bowl. Lamb placed on one side. Pipe 2–3 dots labneh. Scatter pomegranate seeds. Place flatbread crisps on top. Dust with remaining sumac.
service: Dress and assemble to order or in small batches of 5 — flatbread softens within 5–8 min once in contact with dressed salad.
Labneh in piping bag for clean, consistent dots — squeeze bag from coldroom just before service.
Lamb can be cooked 30–40 min ahead and held at 60°C in bain marie.
Pomegranate seeds scattered last — they bleed colour into the salad if left to sit.
note: Sumac is non-negotiable to fattoush character — do not substitute. Pomegranate seeds are both visual and textural; do not omit. Flatbread must be fully crisp before service — any softness will accelerate wilting in the bowl. For lamb mince, retain some larger chunks rather than fine crumble — gives better texture in the bowl. Flag: no Kuschi exclusions present.

name: Mediterranean Chicken Bowl
subtitle: Grilled Sumac Chicken · Tabbouleh · Tzatziki · Flatbread Crisps
label: Mediterranean Bowl

ingredients.item: Bulgur Wheat — fine or medium grain

ingredients.prep: fine chop — leaves and tender stems only

method_steps: MARINADE: Combine olive oil, sumac, oregano, microplaned garlic, lemon juice and salt. Coat chicken thighs. Marinate minimum 1 hour refrigerated — overnight preferred for deeper flavour.

BULGUR: Rinse bulgur. Cook in 2x volume of salted boiling water, 10–12 min until tender. Drain, spread on a tray and cool to room temperature completely before folding through herbs — hot bulgur will wilt parsley and discolour.

TABBOULEH: Fold finely chopped parsley, mint and spring onion through cooled bulgur. Dress with lemon juice and EVOO. Season. Tabbouleh should be parsley-dominant — ratio is 2 bunches parsley to 200 g raw bulgur (approx. 550 g cooked). Taste and adjust lemon.
FLATBREAD: Toss torn flatbread in olive oil. Spread on lined tray. Bake at 190°C fan-forced 8–10 min until golden and crisp. Cool completely on rack.
CHICKEN: Grill on preheated char grill or flat grill at medium-high heat, 5–6 min each side. Check internal temperature — 74°C at thickest point. Rest 5 min. Slice on the bias to 5–6 mm.
BOWL BUILD: Base of tabbouleh. Sliced chicken placed to one side. Dollop or pipe 25–30 ml tzatziki. Flatbread crisps balanced on top. Dust sumac over chicken and flatbread.
service: Tabbouleh can be fully dressed and held refrigerated up to 2 hours — lemon continues to season the bulgur and improves with a short rest.
Assemble bowls at service — do not pre-build more than 10 min ahead as flatbread softens.
Tzatziki can be in a piping bag for speed or served in a small jug on the side.
Chicken can be grilled up to 45 min ahead, sliced and held in bain marie at 60°C.
note: Bulgur must be fully cooled before folding in herbs — this is the most common error; warm grain wilts parsley and turns tabbouleh muddy. Tabbouleh is parsley-dominant by definition — do not allow bulgur to take over. Tzatziki made in-house should use strained yoghurt, grated and squeezed cucumber (raw cucumber only used in tzatziki as a minor processed component — not a key ingredient under Kuschi exclusions), garlic, lemon and dill. Flag: check tzatziki — raw cucumber is a minor component in tzatziki only; if Kuschi's exclusion applies strictly, substitute tzatziki with labneh and herb oil.

name: Beef Lasagne with Side Salad
subtitle: House Bolognese · Béchamel · Pasta Sheets · Dressed Leaves
label: Beef Lasagne

ingredients.item: Beef Mince — minimum 20% fat for flavour

ingredients.prep: for browning mince and sweating vegetables
finely grated — 150 g into béchamel, 100 g for top
if dried, use no-boil style or pre-cook 4 min in salted water

method_steps: BOLOGNESE: Heat olive oil in a heavy-based pot over high heat. Brown mince in batches — do not crowd the pan or it will steam rather than brown. Develop deep colour on each batch before removing. Set aside. Reduce heat to medium. Sweat onion, carrot, celery and garlic in same pot until softened, 8–10 min. Add tomato paste, cook out 2–3 min stirring until paste darkens. Return mince. Deglaze with red wine, scraping base. Reduce wine by half, 3–4 min. Add passata and bouquet garni. Stir to combine. Simmer uncovered on low heat 45–60 min until sauce is thick and rich. Remove bouquet garni. Season well.

BÉCHAMEL: Melt butter in a heavy-based saucepan over medium heat. Add flour all at once. Cook roux, stirring constantly, 2 min until it smells nutty — do not brown. Add warm milk gradually, whisking continuously to prevent lumps. Bring to a gentle simmer, stir until thickened and coats the back of a spoon, 8–10 min. Fold in 150 g grated parmesan. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg. Béchamel should be pourable but not watery — it will thicken further on baking.

ASSEMBLE LASAGNE: Use a deep gastronorm tray (1/1 size, 100 mm deep). Spread a thin layer of béchamel on the base to prevent pasta sticking. Layer: bolognese, pasta sheets, béchamel. Repeat 3–4 layers finishing with béchamel on top. Scatter remaining 100 g parmesan over top.

BAKE: Preheat oven to 180°C conventional. Cover tray tightly with foil. Bake 25 min covered, then remove foil and bake a further 15–20 min until top is deep golden and bubbling at edges. Total 40–45 min. Internal temperature should reach 85°C.
REST: Remove from oven. Rest uncovered minimum 15 min before cutting — this is non-negotiable for clean portions. If made ahead, cool fully, refrigerate, and re-heat at 170°C covered for 25–30 min.
SALAD: Dress mixed leaves with lemon vinaigrette at service — toss to coat lightly. Do not pre-dress.
service: Portion lasagne with a sharp knife into equal squares — a 4x5 grid gives 20 portions from a 1/1 tray.
Use a fish slice or palette knife to lift portions cleanly — slide, do not drag.
Lasagne holds well in bain marie at 75°C for up to 45 min after portioning. Cover to prevent drying.
Dress and plate side salad to order — pre-dressed leaves wilt within 10 min.
Serve with a wedge of lemon on the side if desired.
note: Resting the lasagne is critical — cutting too early causes it to collapse and service becomes messy. Bolognese can and should be made the day before; flavour improves overnight. Béchamel quantity here (100 g butter / 100 g flour / 1.2 L milk) is a slightly richer ratio than the original — the additional liquid compensates for pasta absorption during baking and prevents a dry lasagne. Passata gives a lighter, cleaner sauce than canned tomatoes — correct for this style and yield size. Flag: recipe contains tomato (passata and tomato paste) as structural ingredients — confirm whether Kuschi's tomato exclusion applies to cooked/sauce form, as this would require a fundamental recipe change.

name: Breakfast Wraps
subtitle: Scrambled Egg · Bacon · Cheese · Chipotle Mayo
label: Breakfast Wraps
method_steps: CHIPOTLE MAYO: Blitz mayonnaise, chipotle in adobo, and lemon juice until smooth. Season with salt and white pepper. Hold chilled.
BACON: Grill or pan-fry short cut bacon over medium-high heat until crispy. Drain on absorbent paper. Hold warm.

EGGS: Whisk eggs with cream and a pinch of salt. Melt butter in a heavy-based pan over low heat. Add eggs and cook low and slow, folding gently with a spatula every 30 seconds until large, soft curds just set — remove from heat while still slightly underdone; residual heat will finish them. Season.
WARM TORTILLAS: Warm tortillas in a dry pan over medium heat for 20–30 seconds per side, or stack and wrap in foil and place in conventional oven at 150°C for 5 min.
ASSEMBLE: Lay tortilla flat. Spread 1 tbsp chipotle mayo across centre. Add a portion of scrambled egg, 1 rasher bacon, and a pinch of grated cheddar. Roll tightly, folding in sides.
HOLD: Wrap each roll individually in foil. Hold in kick oven at 70°C for up to 30 min.
service: Serve warm, foil on until pass — guests unwrap at the table or foil removed at pass.
For platter service, cut diagonally on the bias and display cut-side up.
note: Scrambled eggs must be soft and custardy — pull from heat just before fully set. If making ahead, wrap assembled rolls in foil and reheat in conventional oven at 160°C for 8 min. Do not reheat in microwave — tortilla toughens.

name: Gourmet Filled Cookies
subtitle: Chocolate · Vanilla Cream · Caramel
label: Gourmet Cookies

ingredients.item: Gourmet Cookies — house bake or quality supplier

ingredients.prep: melted over bain marie, held at 45°C

method_steps: COOKIES: Bake in-house or source from quality bakery supplier on day of event. Cool completely on a rack before filling.

VANILLA CREAM: Combine cold thickened cream, sifted icing sugar, and vanilla extract in a chilled bowl. Whip to firm peaks using a hand mixer or stand mixer. Transfer to a piping bag with a star tip. Hold chilled.
CARAMEL COOKIES: Spoon or pipe approximately 8–10 g of caramel or dulce de leche onto the centre of each allocated cookie.
CHOCOLATE: Melt dark chocolate over a bain marie to 45°C. Dip cookie edges or drizzle with a fork. Set on a wire rack at room temperature — do not refrigerate to set or chocolate will bloom.
PLATE: Arrange a mixed selection on platters at service. Dust with icing sugar immediately before going to pass.
service: Serve at room temperature. Dust with icing sugar at pass, not ahead of time.
Keep covered until service — cream-filled cookies dry out quickly. Remove covers no more than 15 min before guests arrive.
note: Source from a quality bakery supplier if not making in-house. Cream-filled cookies must be held in the coldroom until 20 min before service. Caramel and chocolate varieties are stable at room temperature for up to 2 hours.

name: Mixed Filled Sandwiches
subtitle: Assorted Fillings · White & Wholemeal · Finger Cut
label: Mixed Sandwiches

ingredients.prep: poached in seasoned water, cooled, finely sliced or pulled
thinly sliced on mandoline or deli slicer

method_steps: CHICKEN FILLING: Combine pulled or sliced poached chicken with mayonnaise. Season with salt and white pepper. Mix until evenly coated. Hold chilled.
BREAD PREP: Lay out bread slices in pairs. Butter all slices edge to edge with softened butter — this seals bread and prevents filling from soaking through.
FILLING 1 — CHICKEN MAYO: Spread chicken mayo generously on base slice (approx 60–70 g per sandwich). Top with a layer of baby spinach. Close and press firmly.
FILLING 2 — CHEDDAR & MUSTARD: Spread mustard on base slice. Layer cheddar slices to cover. Top with baby spinach. Close and press firmly.
CUTTING: Using a sharp serrated knife, trim any remaining crust. Cut each sandwich into 3 even fingers. Wipe blade between cuts for clean edges.
HOLD: Arrange by filling type on a tray. Cover tightly with cling film. Refrigerate until service.
service: Remove cling film and plate at service. Arrange alternating bread types and fillings on platter.
If holding on pass for more than 20 min, cover with a lightly dampened clean cloth to prevent edges drying.
note: Minimum 3 filling types across the batch — vary across white and wholemeal for presentation. Label each tray clearly by filling type. Chicken filling must remain chilled at all times; do not hold at room temperature for more than 30 min.

name: Roasted Vegetable Salad Bowl
subtitle: Seasonal Roast Veg · Grains · Tahini Dressing
label: Roast Veg Bowl

ingredients.prep: dry weight — cooked per packet, spread on tray to cool
dry-toasted in pan until golden, cooled

method_steps: VEGETABLES: Preheat conventional oven to 200°C fan-forced. Toss pumpkin, capsicum, zucchini, and red onion in olive oil, smoked paprika, and cumin. Season well. Spread in a single layer across two lined trays — do not crowd. Roast 25–30 min until edges are caramelised and pumpkin is tender. Rotate trays halfway.
GRAINS: Cook freekeh or brown rice per packet instructions in well-salted water. Drain and spread onto a tray to cool rapidly. Season with salt and a drizzle of olive oil to prevent clumping.

TAHINI DRESSING: Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, microplaned garlic, and water until smooth and pourable. Season with salt. Adjust consistency with additional water, 1 tsp at a time — dressing should coat a spoon but pour freely.
SEEDS: Toast pepitas or sunflower seeds in a dry pan over medium heat, tossing constantly, until golden and beginning to pop — approximately 3–4 min. Cool on a flat tray.
BOWL ASSEMBLY: Base of seasoned grains, roasted vegetables arranged on top, drizzle of tahini dressing, scatter toasted seeds, finish with chopped parsley.
service: Assemble bowls or platters at service. Dressing on the side in a jug, or drizzled at the pass — do not pre-dress if holding.
Can be served warm or at room temperature. If serving warm, reheat veg on a tray at 180°C for 8–10 min before assembly.
note: Vegan and GF. Roasted vegetables can be made a day ahead — store in the coldroom, bring to room temperature or reheat before service. Tahini dressing thickens significantly in the coldroom — loosen with water and whisk before service. Label clearly for allergens: sesame (tahini, seeds).

name: Chilli Garlic Prawns & Chorizo
subtitle: King Prawns · Crisp Chorizo · Rocket · Lemon Pan Sauce
label: Prawn & Chorizo

ingredients.prep: peeled, tails on, deveined, patted thoroughly dry on paper towel
5mm rounds or fine dice — consistent size for even rendering
fine slice, not microplane — slice holds texture in high-heat pan
fine slice on bias, seeds removed for controlled heat at canape scale
divided — 20 ml for chorizo, 40 ml for prawns
30 ml juice per batch for deglazing, remaining wedges for service
cold, cubed — for mounting pan sauce
